**Kiosk widget.** Drop a Turntable widget into a marketing page for an auto-rotating 3D product model. Configure it in Widget Studio, copy the iframe embed, and paste it anywhere that accepts HTML ŌĆö WordPress, Webflow, Notion, Framer, Shopify.

**Personal AI companion.** Build an agent with long-term IPFS memory that persists across sessions. Give it a custom voice, a distinctive avatar, and a detailed persona in `instructions.md`. Users can speak to it via microphone and it will remember past conversations.

**3D asset validator.** Use the `validate-model` skill to build an embeddable asset review tool. Drop a GLB onto the viewer; the agent runs the glTF validator, summarizes issues by severity, and tells you what to fix.

**Multi-agent scene.** Use `AgentStageElement` (also exported from `lib.js`) to host multiple agents in a shared scene. Agents can be scripted to interact with each other via the JS API ŌĆö `say()`, `lookAt()`, `play()` ŌĆö composing character-driven experiences.

---

## How It Compares

### vs. `<model-viewer>` (Google)

[`<model-viewer>`](https://modelviewer.dev) is a solid, well-maintained web component for displaying glTF models with AR support. It excels at simple 3D display and is the right choice if that's all you need. three.ws adds the entire AI agent stack on top: LLM runtime, skill system, memory, voice I/O, emotion system, and on-chain identity. It also adds Widget Studio for no-code embeds and a manifest format for portable agent definitions. If you want a talking, interactive character rather than a static viewer, three.ws is the right tool.

### vs. General LLM Chatbots

General-purpose chat interfaces (embedded or hosted) are text-first. They have no awareness of 3D space, no avatar, and no ability to control a scene. three.ws's agent layer is purpose-built to bridge LLM outputs to three.js operations: the model can trigger animations, adjust camera position, highlight hotspots, and express emotions through the avatar. It also has native glTF understanding ŌĆö the LLM can reason about the loaded model's structure via the validator tool.

### vs. Unity / Unreal WebGL Exports

Game engine WebGL exports ship a large runtime (~10ŌĆō50 MB) and require their own asset pipeline. They are not designed to be embedded as lightweight web components. three.ws is browser-native: no game engine, no plugin, no proprietary format. The CDN bundle is compact, the element self-initializes with a script tag, and the viewer runs on standard WebGL 2.0 available in every modern browser. The trade-off is that three.ws is not a general-purpose game engine ŌĆö it is optimized for avatar-centric, interactive AI agent experiences.

---

## Quick Start

The minimum to get a three.ws on screen is two lines of HTML:

```html
<script type="module" src="https://three.ws/agent-3d/latest/agent-3d.js"></script>
<agent-3d body="https://example.com/avatar.glb"></agent-3d>
```

This loads your GLB file into a viewer with orbit controls, auto-sized to its container. No server, no build step, no API key required for a basic viewer.

To add an AI brain:

```html
<agent-3d
  body="https://example.com/avatar.glb"
  brain="claude-sonnet-4-6"
  key-proxy="/api/llm"
  instructions="You are Aria, a helpful product guide."
  voice
></agent-3d>
```

The `key-proxy` attribute points to a serverless function that injects your API key ŌĆö the key never touches the client. The `voice` attribute enables microphone input and TTS output.

For a full step-by-step walkthrough, see the [Quick Start guide](./quick-start.md).

---

## What's Next

Once you have the basics working, the following guides go deeper:

- **[Quick Start](./quick-start.md)** ŌĆö step-by-step from zero to a talking agent
- **[Embedding Guide](./embedding.md)** ŌĆö all attributes, modes, events, and the JS API
- **[Widget Types](./widgets.md)** ŌĆö Turntable, Animation Gallery, Talking Agent, Passport, Hotspot Tour
- **[Agent System Overview](./agent-system.md)** ŌĆö runtime, skill registry, memory, emotion system
- **[Manifest Reference](./agent-manifest.md)** ŌĆö full JSON schema with examples
- **[REST API](./api-reference.md)** and **[JavaScript API](./js-api.md)** ŌĆö backend and in-browser surfaces
- **[Agents vs. avatars](./agents-vs-avatars.md)** ŌĆö the difference, and when to care

If you want to look at what's already in the hosted platform ŌĆö browsing agents, using Widget Studio, or registering on-chain ŌĆö visit [https://three.ws](https://three.ws).

---

<a id="sec-164"></a>

> **Source:** `docs/irl/THREAT-MODEL.md` ┬Ę [Ōåæ table of contents](#table-of-contents)

# IRL nearby-read ŌĆö threat model (H7)

The `/irl` world lets anyone place a 3D agent at a real GPS spot and lets a passer-by
discover it in AR. The single most sensitive thing the platform does here is reveal
**where an agent is**. This document states, honestly, what the public nearby read
protects against, how it degrades, and the residual exposure we accept by design.

The one read in scope: `GET /api/irl/pins?lat&lng&radius` ŌĆö the per-viewer proximity
feed. It is the **only** surface that ever returns another user's agent location.
(`/api/irl/pins/mine`, `agent-card`, `agent-summary`, `interactions*`, `report`,
`share-frame` do not ŌĆö see `reports/irl-location-leak-audit.md`.)

## What the read protects against

| Threat | Control | Where |
|---|---|---|
| **Remote browsing** ("show me agents in Tokyo from my couch") | The caller must send their **own** `lat`/`lng`; results are filtered to `distance_m <= radius`. There is no bbox/window/roster feed and no realtime pin broadcast. | `api/irl/pins.js` nearby branch; `multiplayer/src/rooms/IrlRoom.js` (syncs no pins) |
| **Wide-radius scrape** | `radius` is clamped to `[10, 60]` m server-side; a present-but-non-finite radius is rejected (no NaN box). | `api/irl/pins.js` `Math.min(60, Math.max(10, ŌĆ”))` |
| **Bulk grid harvest** | Per-IP rate limit on every read; **fail-closed** if the limiter can't decide (deny, never an unmetered window). Distinct-cell **sweep detection** fires a deduped, coordinate-free ops alert when one caller reads many geocells in a short window. | `limitFailClosedRead`, `recordCellRead` in `api/irl/pins.js`; `limits.publicIp` in `api/_lib/rate-limit.js` |
| **De-anonymization** (who placed this agent?) | The public projection is an explicit allow-list: `user_id` / `device_token` are never returned; only an `is_mine` boolean computed server-side. | `api/irl/pins.js` nearby projection |
| **Coordinate fingerprinting** | Returned coordinates are coarsened to 5 decimals (~1.1 m, finer than GPS error but stripped of the false-precision tail). The room origin is coarsened too; exact intra-room layout rides relative offsets, not absolute coords. | `roundCoord`, `PUBLIC_COORD_DP` in `api/irl/pins.js` |
| **Credential / position leak via logs** | `req.url` (which carries `lat`/`lng`/`deviceToken`) is redacted before any log/Sentry/Telegram sink. The sweep alert carries only a SHA-256 IP hash + a count ŌĆö never a coordinate, IP, or geocell. | `redactUrl` in `api/_lib/http.js`; `recordCellRead` alert payload |
| **Stale / abusive pins surfacing** | `hidden_at IS NULL` (community-moderation hide at 3 distinct reporters) and `expires_at` (anon pins 7-day) filter every read path. | `api/irl/pins.js`, `api/irl/report.js` |

## Degradation behaviour (no silent fail-open)

- **Limiter degraded / throws on the read** ŌåÆ **fail closed**: the read returns a
  retryable `rate_limiter_unavailable` (HTTP 429), surfaced to the client as
  "temporarily unavailable, retrying." It never serves an unmetered read. The
  backing bucket (`limits.publicIp`) is an in-memory `local` limiter that never
  touches Redis, so it does not throw in practice; `limitFailClosedRead` makes the
  guarantee explicit and asserted (`tests/api/irl-pins-hardening.test.js`).
- **Write limiter degraded** (place/edit/delete) ŌåÆ **fail open**: the DB density,
  per-owner, and report-dedup caps still bound writes, so an infra hiccup never
  blocks a legitimate placement. This asymmetry is deliberate: a blind limiter on a
  *write* is bounded downstream; a blind limiter on the *location read* is not.
- **Sweep detection / ops alert** is best-effort: it is fire-and-forget, wrapped so
  it can never delay or fail the read, and degrades to "no alert" if the cache or
  Telegram is unavailable. It never widens or narrows what the read returns.

## Accepted residual exposure (by design ŌĆö this is the product)

- **A person physically standing at a spot can see the handful of pins right where
  they stand.** That is the entire feature: you discover an agent in AR by being
  next to it. Within the Ōēż60 m radius, a co-located caller learns those pins'
  coarsened coordinates. We do not consider this a leak ŌĆö it is the product working.
- **A determined attacker who physically walks a grid** can, cell by cell, see each
  cell's pins as they enter it (a real fix per cell, slowly, tripping neither the
  per-minute limit at low rate nor ŌĆö if patient enough ŌĆö necessarily the sweep
  threshold). The sweep detector raises the cost and surfaces the pattern to ops, but
  physical presence is, by definition, allowed to see what is physically present.
  We accept this; the data exposed is still coarsened, owner-stripped, and bounded to
  cells the attacker actually visited.
- **Coordinate precision is ~1.1 m, not exact.** This is a deliberate coverage cap:
  the public read intentionally returns less precision than the placement stored.
  Room-anchored agents keep exact intra-room layout via relative offsets, so the cap
  costs render quality nothing while removing the false-precision fingerprint.

## Out of scope here

- Proof-of-presence (H3) ŌĆö a cell-bound fix token would further bind a read to a
  real, recent fix in that cell, denying a token-per-cell sweep. If/when it lands,
  the cross-cell denial assertion belongs in `tests/api/irl-pins-hardening.test.js`.
- Per-account reputation / banning of repeat sweepers (future).

No limit in this document silently reduces coverage: every cap above is written down.
If a future change tightens the radius, the rate limit, or the coordinate precision
in a way that drops legitimate discovery, update this file in the same change.

---

<a id="sec-165"></a>

> **Source:** `docs/js-api.md` ┬Ę [Ōåæ table of contents](#table-of-contents)

# JavaScript API Reference

Complete reference for all public classes, methods, and properties exported by the three.ws web component bundle (`agent-3d.js`).

> For the HTTP/REST surface, see [api-reference.md](./api-reference.md). For the high-level npm SDK, see [sdk.md](./sdk.md). For the `<agent-3d>` element attributes, see [web-component.md](./web-component.md).

---

## Table of Contents

- [App](#app)
- [Viewer](#viewer)
- [Validator](#validator)
- [Components](#components)
- [Environments](#environments)
- [Global State](#global-state)

---

## App

**File:** `src/app.js`

The top-level application controller. Manages user interaction (drag-and-drop, file upload), URL parameter parsing, and orchestrates the `Viewer` and `Validator`.

### Constructor

```javascript
new App(el: Element, location: Location)
```

| Parameter  | Type       | Description                                        |
| ---------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `el`       | `Element`  | Root DOM element (typically `document.body`)       |
| `location` | `Location` | Browser location object for hash parameter parsing |

Parses the following URL hash parameters via `query-string`:

| Hash Param       | Type      | Default             | Description                                       |
| ---------------- | --------- | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `model`          | `string`  | `'/avatars/cz.glb'` | URL of a glTF/GLB model to load                   |
| `kiosk`          | `boolean` | `false`             | Hides header; intended for iframe embedding       |
| `preset`         | `string`  | `''`                | `'assetgenerator'` activates asset generator mode |
| `cameraPosition` | `string`  | `null`              | Comma-separated `x,y,z` camera coordinates        |

### Properties

| Property            | Type              | Description                                   |
| ------------------- | ----------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `el`                | `Element`         | Root DOM element                              |
| `viewer`            | `Viewer \| null`  | Viewer instance (created on first model load) |
| `viewerEl`          | `Element \| null` | Viewer container element                      |
| `spinnerEl`         | `Element`         | Loading spinner element                       |
| `dropEl`            | `Element`         | Drop target element (`.wrap`)                 |
| `inputEl`           | `Element`         | Hidden file input element                     |
| `viewerContainerEl` | `Element`         | Container for the 3D viewport                 |
| `validator`         | `Validator`       | Validator instance                            |
| `options`           | `object`          | Parsed URL hash options                       |

### Methods

#### `createDropzone()`

Sets up the [simple-dropzone](https://github.com/donmccurdy/simple-dropzone) controller on the `.wrap` element. Listens for:

- `drop` ŌåÆ calls `this.load(files)`
- `dropstart` ŌåÆ shows spinner
- `droperror` ŌåÆ hides spinner

#### `createViewer() ŌåÆ Viewer`

Instantiates a new `Viewer` on the `#viewer-container` element. Called once on first model load; subsequent loads reuse the same viewer.

#### `load(fileMap: Map<string, File>)`

Processes a fileset from a drag-and-drop or file input event.

1. Iterates the `fileMap` to find the root `.gltf` or `.glb` file
2. Extracts the `rootPath` (directory portion of the file path)
3. Calls `this.view(rootFile, rootPath, fileMap)`
4. Shows an alert if no `.gltf`/`.glb` file is found

#### `view(rootFile: File | string, rootPath: string, fileMap: Map<string, File>)`

Loads a model into the viewer and runs validation.

| Parameter  | Type                | Description                                   |
| ---------- | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `rootFile` | `File \| string`    | The root glTF/GLB file object or URL string   |
| `rootPath` | `string`            | Directory prefix for resolving relative URIs  |
| `fileMap`  | `Map<string, File>` | All files from the drop (for multi-file glTF) |

Flow:

1. Clears any existing scene from the viewer
2. Creates a blob URL if `rootFile` is a `File` object
3. Calls `Viewer.load()` to render the model
4. Calls `Validator.validate()` (unless in kiosk mode)
5. Revokes the blob URL in the cleanup callback

#### `onError(error: Error | string)`

Normalizes and displays an error message via `window.alert()`. Special case handling:

| Pattern                         | Displayed Message                 |
| ------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| Contains `ProgressEvent`        | "Unable to retrieve this file..." |
| Contains `Unexpected token`     | "Unable to parse file content..." |
| `error.target instanceof Image` | "Missing texture: {filename}"     |

#### `showSpinner()` / `hideSpinner()`

Toggle the loading spinner's `display` style.

---

## Viewer

**File:** `src/viewer.js`

The Three.js rendering engine. Manages the WebGL scene, camera, renderer, controls, lighting, environment maps, animations, morph targets, and the dat.gui panel.

### Constructor

```javascript
new Viewer(el: Element, options: object)
```

| Parameter | Type      | Description                                        |
| --------- | --------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `el`      | `Element` | Container element for the WebGL canvas             |
| `options` | `object`  | Options from `App` (kiosk, preset, cameraPosition) |

The constructor:

1. Creates `WebGLRenderer`, `PerspectiveCamera`, `Scene`, `OrbitControls`
2. Initializes `PMREMGenerator` for environment map processing
3. Creates the neutral environment from `THREE.RoomEnvironment`
4. Sets up the axes helper (mini viewport in bottom-left)
5. Builds the dat.gui panel
6. Starts the render loop via `requestAnimationFrame`
7. Binds the `resize` event

### Properties

| Property             | Type                           | Description                                                 |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `el`                 | `Element`                      | Container element                                           |
| `options`            | `object`                       | Configuration options                                       |
| `scene`              | `THREE.Scene`                  | The Three.js scene                                          |
| `defaultCamera`      | `THREE.PerspectiveCamera`      | Default orbit camera                                        |
| `activeCamera`       | `THREE.Camera`                 | Currently active camera (default or embedded)               |
| `renderer`           | `THREE.WebGLRenderer`          | WebGL renderer (also on `window.renderer`)                  |
| `controls`           | `OrbitControls`                | Orbit controls instance                                     |
| `content`            | `THREE.Object3D \| null`       | Currently loaded model root                                 |
| `mixer`              | `THREE.AnimationMixer \| null` | Animation mixer                                             |
| `clips`              | `THREE.AnimationClip[]`        | Animation clips from the loaded model                       |
| `lights`             | `THREE.Light[]`                | App-provided lights (ambient + directional)                 |
| `gui`                | `GUI`                          | dat.gui instance                                            |
| `state`              | `object`                       | Current GUI state (see [State Object](#state-object) below) |
| `stats`              | `Stats`                        | stats.js performance monitor                                |
| `backgroundColor`    | `THREE.Color`                  | Current background color                                    |
| `pmremGenerator`     | `THREE.PMREMGenerator`         | Environment map processor                                   |
| `neutralEnvironment` | `THREE.Texture`                | Pre-computed neutral environment                            |
| `skeletonHelpers`    | `THREE.SkeletonHelper[]`       | Active skeleton overlays                                    |
| `gridHelper`         | `THREE.GridHelper \| null`     | Grid overlay                                                |
| `axesHelper`         | `THREE.AxesHelper \| null`     | Axes overlay in main scene                                  |
| `axesScene`          | `THREE.Scene`                  | Mini axes viewport scene                                    |
| `axesCamera`         | `THREE.PerspectiveCamera`      | Mini axes viewport camera                                   |
| `axesRenderer`       | `THREE.WebGLRenderer`          | Mini axes viewport renderer                                 |
| `axesCorner`         | `THREE.AxesHelper`             | Axes object in mini viewport                                |

### State Object

The `this.state` object holds all GUI-controllable values:

```javascript
{
    environment: 'Neutral',              // Environment map name
    background: false,                   // Show environment as background
    playbackSpeed: 1.0,                  // Animation playback speed (0ŌĆō1)
    actionStates: {},                    // Per-clip play state { clipName: bool }
    camera: '[default]',                 // Active camera name
    wireframe: false,                    // Wireframe rendering
    skeleton: false,                     // Skeleton helper visibility
    grid: false,                         // Grid + axes visibility
    autoRotate: false,                   // Auto-rotate orbit

    // Lighting
    punctualLights: true,                // App-provided lights enabled
    exposure: 0.0,                       // Exposure compensation (EV)
    toneMapping: LinearToneMapping,      // Tone mapping mode
    ambientIntensity: 0.3,               // Ambient light intensity
    ambientColor: '#FFFFFF',             // Ambient light color
    directIntensity: 0.8 * Math.PI,     // Directional light intensity
    directColor: '#FFFFFF',              // Directional light color
    bgColor: '#191919',                  // Background color

    pointSize: 1.0,                      // Point cloud vertex size
}
```

### Methods

#### `animate(time: number)`

Main render loop callback. Called every frame via `requestAnimationFrame`.

- Updates OrbitControls
- Updates stats.js
- Advances AnimationMixer by delta time
- Calls `this.render()`

#### `render()`

Renders the main scene and the axes helper mini-viewport.

#### `resize()`

Handles window resize. Updates camera aspect ratio, renderer size, and axes renderer size.

#### `load(url: string, rootPath: string, assetMap: Map<string, File>) ŌåÆ Promise<GLTF>`

Loads a glTF/GLB model.

| Parameter  | Type                | Description                                  |
| ---------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `url`      | `string`            | URL to the model file (or blob URL)          |
| `rootPath` | `string`            | Directory prefix for resolving relative URIs |
| `assetMap` | `Map<string, File>` | Dropped files for local resource resolution  |

Returns a Promise that resolves with the parsed glTF object. The method:

1. Installs a URL modifier on the `LoadingManager` to intercept relative URIs and serve them from `assetMap` as blob URLs
2. Configures `GLTFLoader` with Draco, KTX2, and Meshopt decoders
3. On success, calls `setContent()` with the scene and animation clips
4. Exports the raw glTF JSON to `window.VIEWER.json`

#### `setContent(object: THREE.Object3D, clips: THREE.AnimationClip[])`

Adds a loaded model to the scene.

1. Calls `this.clear()` to remove any existing model
2. Computes bounding box and centers the model at origin
3. Configures camera near/far planes and position based on model size
4. Respects `options.cameraPosition` if provided
5. Saves initial OrbitControls state
6. Detects embedded lights (sets `state.punctualLights = false` if found)
7. Sets up animation clips via `setClips()`
8. Updates lighting, GUI, environment, and display
9. Prints the scene graph to the console
10. Exports the scene to `window.VIEWER.scene`

#### `setClips(clips: THREE.AnimationClip[])`

Replaces the current animation clips. Stops and disposes the existing `AnimationMixer` if one exists, then creates a new one.

#### `playAllClips()`

Plays all animation clips simultaneously by calling `mixer.clipAction(clip).reset().play()` on each.

#### `setCamera(name: string)`

Switches the active camera.

| Value            | Behavior                                                                           |
| ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `'[default]'`    | Activates the orbit camera; enables OrbitControls                                  |
| Any other string | Traverses the scene for a camera with `node.name === name`; disables OrbitControls |

#### `updateLights()`

Synchronizes light state with the GUI:

- Adds or removes punctual lights based on `state.punctualLights`
- Sets `renderer.toneMapping` and `renderer.toneMappingExposure`
- Updates ambient/directional light intensity and color

#### `addLights()`

Creates and adds the ambient + directional light pair (or a single hemisphere light in asset generator mode).

#### `removeLights()`

Removes all app-provided lights from the scene and empties the `lights` array.

#### `updateEnvironment()`

Loads the selected environment map and applies it to `scene.environment`. If `state.background` is true, also sets it as `scene.background`.

#### `getCubeMapTexture(environment: object) ŌåÆ Promise<{envMap: THREE.Texture}>`

Processes an environment definition into a usable environment map:

| Environment ID | Processing                                                       |
| -------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `'neutral'`    | Returns pre-computed `RoomEnvironment` texture                   |
| `''`           | Returns `null` (no environment)                                  |
| Any other      | Loads `.exr` via `EXRLoader`, processes through `PMREMGenerator` |

#### `updateDisplay()`

Applies display state changes:

- Wireframe mode on all materials
- Point size for `PointsMaterial`
- Skeleton helpers on skinned meshes
- Grid + axes helpers
- Auto-rotate on OrbitControls

#### `updateBackground()`

Updates `backgroundColor` from `state.bgColor`.

#### `addAxesHelper()`

Creates the mini axes viewport:

- 100├Ś100 px `<div>` in the bottom-left corner
- Separate `WebGLRenderer` with transparent background
- `AxesHelper(5)` scaled to match the loaded model

#### `addGUI()`

Builds the entire dat.gui panel. On mobile (Ōēż 700 px), uses 220 px width and starts closed; on desktop, 260 px width and starts open.

See [Architecture overview](./architecture.md) for the full folder tree.

#### `updateGUI()`

Rebuilds the dynamic GUI folders (Animation, Morph Targets, Cameras) based on the currently loaded model. Called by `setContent()`.

- Removes all previous dynamic controls
- Traverses the model to discover morph target meshes and embedded cameras
- Auto-plays the first animation clip
- Creates per-clip checkboxes and per-morph-target sliders

#### `clear()`

Removes the current model from the scene and disposes all resources:

- Disposes all geometries
- Disposes all textures (except `envMap`)
- Disposes all materials

#### `printGraph(node: THREE.Object3D)`

Recursively prints the scene graph to the console using nested `console.group()` calls. Each line shows `<NodeType> name`.

---

## Validator

**File:** `src/validator.js`

Integrates the [Khronos glTF-Validator](https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF-Validator) and renders validation results.

### Constructor

```javascript
new Validator(el: Element)
```

| Parameter | Type      | Description                                   |
| --------- | --------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `el`      | `Element` | Root DOM element for appending the toggle bar |

### Properties

| Property   | Type             | Description                  |
| ---------- | ---------------- | ---------------------------- |
| `el`       | `Element`        | Root DOM element             |
| `report`   | `object \| null` | Last validation report       |
| `toggleEl` | `Element`        | Container for the toggle bar |

### Methods

#### `validate(rootFile: string, rootPath: string, assetMap: Map<string, File>, response: object) ŌåÆ Promise`

Runs the glTF validator against a loaded model.

| Parameter  | Type                | Description                                  |
| ---------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `rootFile` | `string`            | URL of the model file                        |
| `rootPath` | `string`            | Directory prefix for resolving relative URIs |
| `assetMap` | `Map<string, File>` | Dropped files for local resource resolution  |
| `response` | `object`            | The parsed GLTF object from GLTFLoader       |

Flow:

1. Fetches the model URL as an `ArrayBuffer`
2. Calls `validateBytes()` from `gltf-validator`
3. Provides `externalResourceFunction` for resolving external resources
4. Passes the result to `setReport()`

#### `resolveExternalResource(uri: string, rootFile: string, rootPath: string, assetMap: Map<string, File>) ŌåÆ Promise<Uint8Array>`

Resolves an external resource (texture, bin) referenced by the glTF during validation.

1. Normalizes the URI by decoding and removing the base URL
2. Checks `assetMap` for a local match ŌåÆ creates blob URL
3. Falls back to a network fetch
4. Returns the resource as a `Uint8Array`

#### `setReport(report: object, response: object)`

Processes the raw validator report:

1. Extracts the generator string from `report.info.generator`
2. Determines `maxSeverity` (lowest severity index with > 0 messages)
3. Splits messages into `errors[]`, `warnings[]`, `infos[]`, `hints[]`
4. Aggregates high-frequency messages (`ACCESSOR_NON_UNIT`, `ACCESSOR_ANIMATION_INPUT_NON_INCREASING`)
5. Extracts `asset.extras` metadata (author, license, source, title) from the GLTF response
6. Renders `ValidatorToggle` HTML into the toggle element

#### `setResponse(response: object)`

Extracts metadata from the glTF `asset.extras` field:

| Extra     | Processing                  |
| --------- | --------------------------- |
| `author`  | HTML-escaped then linkified |
| `license` | HTML-escaped then linkified |
| `source`  | HTML-escaped then linkified |
| `title`   | Stored as-is                |

#### `setReportException(e: Error)`

Called when validation fails. Sets report to `null` and renders an error message in the toggle.

#### `bindListeners()`

Binds click event on the toggle bar to open the lightbox report, and the close button to dismiss it.

#### `showToggle()` / `hideToggle()`

Add/remove the `hidden` CSS class on the toggle element.

#### `showLightbox()`

Opens a new browser tab with the full validation report HTML (rendered via `ValidatorReport` component).

---

## Components

All components are pure functions that return HTML strings via [vhtml](https://github.com/developit/vhtml) JSX.

### `Footer()`

**File:** `src/components/footer.jsx`

Returns a `<footer>` with:

- X (Twitter) link to [@nichxbt](https://x.com/trythreews)
- "help & feedback" link to GitHub issues
- GitHub repository link
- Pipe separators

**No props.**

### `ValidatorToggle({ issues, reportError })`

**File:** `src/components/validator-toggle.jsx`

Renders the validation status bar.

| Prop          | Type                  | Description                       |
| ------------- | --------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| `issues`      | `object \| undefined` | Validation issues summary         |
| `reportError` | `Error \| undefined`  | Error if validation failed to run |

Message logic:

- `numErrors > 0` ŌåÆ "X errors."
- `numWarnings > 0` ŌåÆ "X warnings."
- `numHints > 0` ŌåÆ "X hints."
- `numInfos > 0` ŌåÆ "X notes."
- Otherwise ŌåÆ "Model details"

CSS class `level-{maxSeverity}` controls the color (0 = red, 1 = yellow).

### `ValidatorReport({ info, validatorVersion, issues, errors, warnings, hints, infos })`

**File:** `src/components/validator-report.jsx`

Renders the full validation report.

| Prop               | Type     | Description                                                 |
| ------------------ | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `info`             | `object` | Model info (version, generator, counts, extensions, extras) |
| `validatorVersion` | `string` | glTF-Validator version                                      |
| `issues`           | `object` | Issues summary with counts                                  |
| `errors`           | `array`  | Error messages                                              |
| `warnings`         | `array`  | Warning messages                                            |
| `hints`            | `array`  | Hint messages                                               |
| `infos`            | `array`  | Info messages                                               |

Displays:

- Format version and generator
- Metadata from `asset.extras` (title, author, license, source)
- Stats: draw calls, animations, materials, vertices, triangles
- Extensions used
- Issue tables (one `ValidatorTable` per severity level with messages)

### `ValidatorTable({ title, color, messages })`

**File:** `src/components/validator-table.jsx`

Renders a color-coded table of validation issues.

| Prop       | Type     | Description                                         |
| ---------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `title`    | `string` | Severity title ("Error", "Warning", "Hint", "Info") |
| `color`    | `string` | Header background color (CSS color string)          |
| `messages` | `array`  | Array of `{ code, message, pointer }` objects       |

Columns: Code, Message, Pointer (JSON pointer into the glTF).

---

## Environments

**File:** `src/environments.js`

Exports `environments` ŌĆö an array of environment map definitions.

### Schema

```typescript
interface Environment {
	id: string; // Unique identifier ('' for none, 'neutral' for RoomEnvironment)
	name: string; // Display name in the GUI dropdown
	path: string | null; // URL to the EXR file (null for procedural/none)
	format?: string; // File format (e.g., '.exr')
}
```

### Default Environments

| ID                | Name                       | Source                               |
| ----------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `''`              | None                       | No environment map                   |
| `neutral`         | Neutral                    | `THREE.RoomEnvironment` (procedural) |
| `venice-sunset`   | Venice Sunset              | `venice_sunset_1k.exr` from GCS      |
| `footprint-court` | Footprint Court (HDR Labs) | `footprint_court_2k.exr` from GCS    |

### Adding Custom Environments

Append to the `environments` array in `src/environments.js`:

```javascript
{
    id: 'my-studio',
    name: 'My Studio',
    path: 'https://your-cdn.com/studio_1k.exr',
    format: '.exr',
}
```

Requirements:

- Must be an equirectangular HDR image in `.exr` format
- 1K resolution (1024├Ś512) is recommended for web delivery
- Must be served with CORS headers allowing the app's origin

---

## Global State

The app exports debugging state to `window.VIEWER`:

| Property              | Type             | Set By                       | Description                            |
| --------------------- | ---------------- | ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `window.VIEWER.app`   | `App`            | `app.js` at DOMContentLoaded | Full App instance                      |
| `window.VIEWER.scene` | `THREE.Object3D` | `Viewer.setContent()`        | Current model scene graph              |
| `window.VIEWER.json`  | `GLTF`           | `Viewer.load()`              | Raw parsed glTF object from GLTFLoader |

The WebGL renderer is also available at `window.renderer`.

### Console Usage Examples

```javascript
// List all meshes
window.VIEWER.scene.traverse((n) => n.isMesh && console.log(n.name, n));

// Get renderer stats
window.VIEWER.app.viewer.renderer.info;

// Get current camera position
window.VIEWER.app.viewer.defaultCamera.position.toArray();

// Force play animation clip by index
window.VIEWER.app.viewer.mixer.clipAction(window.VIEWER.app.viewer.clips[0]).play();

// Change background color programmatically
window.VIEWER.app.viewer.state.bgColor = '#ff0000';
window.VIEWER.app.viewer.updateBackground();

// Toggle wireframe
window.VIEWER.app.viewer.state.wireframe = true;
window.VIEWER.app.viewer.updateDisplay();
```

---

<a id="sec-166"></a>

> **Source:** `docs/launch/go-no-go-2026-06-24.md` ┬Ę [Ōåæ table of contents](#table-of-contents)

# three.ws ŌĆö Production Launch Go / No-Go ŌĆö 2026-06-24

**Prepared by:** Prompt 50 (Final Launch Checklist & Go/No-Go gate)
**Date:** 2026-06-24
**Scope:** Consumes the outputs of prompts 01ŌĆō49 and the `docs/audit/*`, `docs/audits/*`,
`docs/security/*` reports they produced. Re-runs the full verification suite, re-checks every
surface, re-verifies money paths, runs the coin-policy final scan, and confirms
security/legal/ops/CI/backups.

---

## Overall recommendation: ­¤¤Ī CONDITIONAL GO ŌĆö **hold launch until the 4 blockers below clear**

The money-moving core, security posture, on-chain parity, and coin policy are **launch-grade**.
But the full suite is **not all green**, and there are real RED items ŌĆö one of them on a **live
production endpoint** and one tied to a **same-day database migration**. None are deep
architectural problems; all are closeable quickly. Until they are closed (and the build/test
suite is re-confirmed in a clean CI/Vercel environment ŌĆö see Blocker 4), this is a **NO-GO**.

When the four blockers are GREEN, this flips to **GO**.

> **Honesty note (per CLAUDE.md "no green-washing"):** this gate was run inside a shared
> multi-agent codespace where (a) other agents were concurrently editing `main`, and (b) the
> local `node_modules` was found **corrupted across ~38 packages** (top-level + transitive
> ESM entry files stripped by an interrupted/concurrent `npm` operation). That corruption
> blocked a fully-green local `vite build`, `check:dist`, and `vitest`/`playwright` run. Where a
> gate could not be run to completion **for that environmental reason**, it is marked
> `BLOCKED (env)` ŌĆö **not** passed. The authoritative build/test environment is Vercel's clean
> `npm install` (`buildCommand: npm run build:vercel`) plus a clean CI lane (which does not yet
> exist ŌĆö see Blocker 4 / Prompt 37).

---

## 1. Verification suite ŌĆö results & evidence

Run on 2026-06-24 in the workspace. Exit codes captured verbatim.

| Gate | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `typecheck` (`tsc -p jsconfig.json`) | Ō£ģ **PASS** (exit 0) | clean, no errors |
| `lint` (`eslint .`) | Ō£ģ **PASS** (exit 0) | 0 errors (warnings only; non-failing) |
| `audit:pages` / `audit-page-index --strict` | Ō£ģ **PASS** (exit 0, after fix) | 118 routes, 231 documented; "every auditable page route is documented" ŌĆö **fixed this pass** (see ┬¦8) |
| `audit:handlers` | Ō£ģ **PASS** | 1174 API handlers export a body |
| `audit:mcp` | Ō£ģ **PASS** | 14 MCP manifests consistent |
| `audit:hidden-guard` | Ō£ģ **PASS** | all 215 pages resolve the `[hidden]` guard |
| `check:images` (strict) | Ō£ģ **PASS** (after fix) | every `src/` `<img>` declares `loading=` ŌĆö **fixed this pass** (see ┬¦8) |
| `build:pages` | Ō£ģ **PASS** | 338 pages / 11 sections; changelog + sitemap + llms regenerated and validated |
| `audit:deploy` | Ō£ģ **PASS** | no committed symlinks, no unsatisfied peers, no undeclared api imports |
| `verify:solana` (parity) | Ō£ģ **PASS** (exit 0) | 10 verified / 0 wrong / 0 unreachable ŌĆö incl. **$THREE mint `FeMbDoX7R1Psc4GEcvJdsbNbZA3bfztcyDCatJVJpump` (token-2022 mint)**, pump bonding-curve/AMM/fees programs, SPL+Token-2022, USDC, WSOL |
| `verify:onchain` (parity) | Ō£ģ **PASS** (exit 0) | 3 sources agree across 22 chains / 51 address slots; ERC-8004 identity/reputation/validation registries live on Base + Base Sepolia |
| `smoke:onchain:ci` | Ō£ģ **PASS** (exit 0) | address parity OK |
| **Coin-policy scan** (repo-wide) | Ō£ģ **CLEAN** (after remediation) | only `$THREE` promoted; 3 foreign example mints in vendored pump docs neutralized this pass (see ┬¦4) |
| **Security audit** (`SECURITY_AUDIT.md`) | Ō£ģ **CLOSED** | 40 findings: 35 fixed, 1 partially-fixed (CSP, mitigated), 1 dual-read (fixed), 1 audited+migrated (fixed), 1 tracked (isolated dep PR). **Zero `open`.** |
| `build` (`vite build`) | ­¤¤Ī **BLOCKED (env)** | Code is build-sound: prebuild fully green, **1499 modules transformed & written to `dist/`**. Every failure was `Failed to resolve entry for package "<pkg>"` on a vendor package whose extracted files were missing on disk (`@mediapipe/tasks-vision`, `lightweight-charts`, `@solana/codecs-numbers`, ŌĆ”) ŌĆö a corrupted local `node_modules`, **not** a repo defect (manifests intact). **Confirm green on Vercel** (Blocker 4). |
| `check:dist` | ŌÅĖ **NOT RUN** | depends on a complete `dist/` (blocked by build above). Confirm on Vercel. |
| `test` (`vitest`) | ­¤ö┤ **RED** | **`branding.test.js` 4/4 fail** (real, pre-existing ŌĆö Blocker 1). Broader `vitest` runner also intermittently failed to boot under the corrupted `node_modules` (`ERR_LOAD_URL`); full suite to be re-run in clean CI. |
| `test:e2e` (`playwright`) | ŌÅĖ **NOT RUN** | requires browser binaries + clean deps; run in clean CI. |
| `smoke:mcp` (vs **prod** `https://three.ws`) | ­¤ö┤ **5/6** | 5 paid endpoints return correct x402 402/401 gates; **`/api/pump-fun-mcp` `get_token_details` (free, public) errors: "Endpoint URL must start with `http:`"** ŌĆö Blocker 2. |
| `smoke:agent-wallet` | ŌŚÉ **6 BLOCKED + 1 fail** | "BLOCKED = built + verified, waiting on funding/credentials" ŌĆö by design without a funded devnet signer; not a code failure. |
| `pump:smoke` | ŌÅĖ **needs `SMOKE_OWNER_KEY`** | gracefully requires a signer key; run against a funded devnet key in CI. |
| `verify:x402` | ŌÅĖ **NOT RUN** | x402 discovery catalog (network); run in CI. Note: x402 verification core independently validated by the security audit. |

---

## 2. Surface readiness verdicts

Source of truth: `docs/audits/FINDINGS.md` (consolidated across the 7 group docs
[01-main](../audits/01-main.md) ŌĆ” [07-learn-blog-legal](../audits/07-learn-blog-legal.md)),
~115 interactive routes audited.

**Tally:** P0 = **0**, P1 Ōēł 70, P2 Ōēł 50, P3 Ōēł 40.

| Surface group | Verdict | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Main (home, discover, gallery, animations, walk, irl, marketplace, collection, skills, community, characters) | Ō£ģ **READY** | Tier-1 stored-XSS in `characters.js` + `home.html` fallback array + marketplace skeleton bug all **fixed**. |
| Build / create (start, create, forge, scene, compose, pose, app, studio, voice, import) | Ō£ģ **READY**, P1 follow-ups | `/create-agent` parse-failure veil and `/create/selfie` silent-catch tracked (P1, non-blocking). |
| Trading & intel (oracle, activity, leaderboard, trending, trades, smart-money, radar, pump-*) | Ō£ģ **READY**, P1 follow-ups | Feed-failure UX cluster (stale/spinning feeds on `/oracle`, `/smart-money`, `/pump-dashboard`, `/pumpfun`, `/pump-visualizer`) tracked ŌĆö copy `/pump-live`'s reconnect pattern. |
| x402 & payments (pay, bazaar, arbitrage, providers, ibm/x402-demo, fact-checker, tutor, club, agent-exchange, agent-economy, three) | Ō£ģ **READY** | Fake-progress + fabricated-bazaar listings **fixed**. Payment plumbing strong (see ┬¦3). |
| Launch & wallets (launch, launches, lookup, vanity-wallet, evm-wallet, play/arena, play/agent-wallet) | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ **READY w/ caveat** | `/play/agent-wallet` "your avatar pays" flow needs a deployed `BRIDGE_URL` (empty for non-localhost) or an honest dev-only state (P1). |
| Labs / agent / account (launchpad, brain, lipsync, playground, chat, agent, my-agents, reputation, login, register, dashboard/*) | Ō£ģ **READY** | Auth (`privy-login.js`) verified wired. |
| Docs / blog / legal / machine (docs/*, tutorials/*, status, glossary, support, blog/*, legal/*) | Ō£ģ **READY**, P2 | Blog-footer regression (~22/28 posts load `footer.css` but don't render footer) tracked (P2). |

**Bottom line:** no P0s; every surface reachable; the dead-link and hard-rule violations from the
audit are resolved. The open items are P1/P2 polish (feed-failure UX, a few mobile/a11y gaps, the
agent-wallet bridge URL) ŌĆö **non-blocking** for launch but should be burned down post-launch.

---

## 3. Money paths ŌĆö re-verified

| Path | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| x402 payment verification | Ō£ģ | Independently decodes signed transfer **amount/recipient/mint server-side** before trusting any facilitator; always-on replay guard (`SECURITY_AUDIT.md`, "Overall posture"). |
| $THREE quote ŌåÆ settle | Ō£ģ | HMAC-signed quotes, timing-safe comparison, on-chain memo binding, unique-nonce/tx replay protection. **$THREE mint live-verified on-chain** (`verify:solana`). |
| Custodial signing / wallet keys | Ō£ģ | Verifies agent ownership before decrypting keys; wallet-key encryption moved to dedicated `WALLET_ENCRYPTION_KEY` + per-record salt, dual-read (no migration). |
| Spend caps / idempotency / OAuth code | Ō£ģ | TOCTOU races closed with `pg_advisory_xact_lock` / atomic NX / atomic consume-or-revoke (H6, H9, M7, M10). |
| Withdrawals | Ō£ģ | Destination resolved from `agent_payout_wallets WHERE user_id` (server-side), not request body (H5). |
| Marketplace / launches entitlements | Ō£ģ | Skill-license verification (DB + on-chain SPL-NFT/PDA) wired; `/launches` renders from platform launch records (runtime data, coin-policy-compliant). |
| No double-charge / correct settlement | Ō£ģ | Per-gated-call settle; single-payment batches with >1 gated call rejected (H7). |

**Money-path verdict: Ō£ģ GO.** The strongest part of the codebase. The one caveat is operational,
not code: settlement/entitlement state lives in the canonical Neon DB ŌĆö see Blocker 3.

---

## 4. Coin policy ŌĆö final scan

**Verdict: Ō£ģ CLEAN (after remediation this pass).**

- `$THREE` (`FeMbDoX7R1Psc4GEcvJdsbNbZA3bfztcyDCatJVJpump`) is the **only** coin referenced/promoted ŌĆö
  262 references across the repo; live-verified on-chain as a token-2022 mint.
- **No `$`-prefixed foreign tickers** in any product UI/code (`src/`, `pages/`, `public/`, `api/`).
- `api/_lib/copy/smart-wallets.json` (gmgn smart-money **trader/wallet** taxonomy) is runtime intel
  **data about wallets/KOLs**, not coin promotion ŌĆö compliant runtime-data plumbing.
- Synthetic placeholders only (`ABCdefGHJKLMNPQRstuvwxyzŌĆ”pump`, and the new
  `EXAMPLEpumpfunBaseMintŌĆ”pump`) ŌĆö explicitly blessed by CLAUDE.md.

**Remediated this pass** (3 real foreign example mints found in **vendored pump.fun protocol docs**
ŌĆö example addresses for PDA bump-seed derivation, not promoted in product, but the coin rule covers
docs and says "remove like a leaked secret"):

| File | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `docs/pumpfun-program/docs/FAQ.md` | `Coyj3ŌĆ”pump`, `3cLSxŌĆ”pump` + their solscan tx links in worked bump-seed examples | Genericized ŌĆö the PDA-bump lesson preserved without naming foreign mints/txs |
| `docs/pumpfun-program/docs/PUMP_SWAP_README.md` | `base_mint: 7LSsEŌĆ”pump` | `base_mint: EXAMPLEpumpfunBaseMintŌĆ”pump` (synthetic) |
| `docs/ALL.md` | mirrored the above | regenerated via `scripts/combine-docs.mjs` |

Post-fix scan: **zero** non-`$THREE` pump-style mints outside synthetic placeholders. **No launch
blocker from coin policy.**

---

## 5. Security ┬Ę legal ┬Ę ops ┬Ę observability ┬Ę CI ┬Ę backups

| Area | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **Security (Prompt 14)** | Ō£ģ **CLOSED** | `SECURITY_AUDIT.md`: 40 findings, **0 open** (35 fixed + mitigated/dual-read/tracked). SSRF guards, 2 stored-XSS sinks, TOCTOU races, header/CSP, wallet-key encryption all closed. 1 tracked item = isolated breaking dep bump (`@metaplex-foundation/js`), scheduled separately. |
| **Legal (Prompt 44)** | Ō£ģ **LIVE** | `public/legal/`: `privacy.html`, `tos.html`, `content-policy.html`, `eula.html`, `aws-marketplace-eula.html`; 7 `/legal/*` rewrites in `vercel.json`. |
| **Observability / health / status (Prompts 36/41)** | Ō£ģ **LIVE** | Sentry (`api/_lib/sentry.js`); health/status endpoints (`api/healthz.js`, `api/status.js`, `api/llm/health.js`, `api/sniper/status.js`, `api/forever/status.js`, `api/x/status.js`); public status page (`pages/status.html`). |
| **CI gates (Prompt 37)** | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ **PARTIAL** | **No `.github/workflows/` and no husky hooks.** Deploy-time gating is enforced **only** via Vercel `buildCommand: npm run build:vercel`, which runs `audit:deploy Ōłź prebuild (build-page-index + audit-page-index --strict) Ōłź audit:mcp`. `lint`, `typecheck`, `test`, `test:e2e`, `check:images`, and the **branding test are NOT gated on push/PR**. ŌåÆ A push CI lane is needed so RED tests like branding (Blocker 1) cannot reach prod silently. |
| **Backups (Prompt 38)** | Ō£ģ **PRESENT** | App-data daily snapshots recoverable from git history (`scripts/snapshot-export-day.sh <date>`); DB on Neon (managed PITR); legacyŌåÆcanonical reconciliation tool `scripts/restore-missing-tables.sh` (data-safe, copies only missing tables). |

---

## 6. Open risks & blockers

### ­¤ö┤ Blockers ŌĆö must be GREEN before GO

1. **Branding test is RED (pre-existing regression).** `tests/branding.test.js` fails 4/4: forbidden
   third-party avatar-platform brand names (multiple occurrences each) in user-facing
   files (e.g. `src/runtime/arkit52.js` ŌĆö confirmed present at HEAD, **not** introduced by this gate;
   import tutorials; AvatarCreator dependency notes). The 2026-06-18 remediation had this green; 6
   days of development reintroduced the leaks without rebranding/allowlisting.
   **Owner:** branding workstream (Prompt 04-branding). **Fix:** per the established pattern ŌĆö
   rebrand marketing copy to three.ws language; add justified entries to
   `tests/branding-allowlist.json` for legitimate technical references (the supported-rig/blendshape
   conventions CLAUDE.md itself sanctions, the documented MIT Character-Studio fork). Re-run
   `npx vitest run tests/branding.test.js` ŌåÆ 0 fail. *(Left to the owning workstream rather than
   risk a 15-location rebrand under concurrent edits during the gate.)*

2. **Live production MCP tool erroring.** `smoke:mcp` against `https://three.ws` shows the **public,
   free** `/api/pump-fun-mcp` tool `get_token_details` returns *"Endpoint URL must start with
   `http:` or `https:`"* (a `@solana/web3.js` Connection rejecting a non-http RPC URL). The latest
   commit (`a0ce03a2a`, "harden Solana RPC fallback and URL guards") targets exactly this ŌĆö so it is
   likely **fix-pending-deploy** or an **unset/invalid prod Solana RPC env var**. **Owner:** pump/MCP
   + ops. **Fix:** confirm the prod RPC env (e.g. `SOLANA_RPC_URL`/Helius) is set, ensure the
   URL-guard fallback provides a valid default rather than throwing, deploy, then `smoke:mcp` ŌåÆ 6/6.

3. **Canonical database migration must be confirmed.** A **Vercel account switch happened TODAY
   (2026-06-24)** moving production to a new Neon DB (`ep-curly-cherry`); the documented incident is a
   "relation ŌĆ” does not exist" error storm (~98% of error volume) when the new DB lacks tables. The
   remediation exists (`scripts/restore-missing-tables.sh`; `npm run db:bootstrap` ŌĆö 4 steps,
   idempotent, "do not skip step 4"). **Migrations are NOT auto-applied on deploy.** **Owner:** ops/DB.
   **Fix (verify before GO):** run `npm run db:status` against the canonical `DATABASE_URL`;
   confirm `restore-missing-tables.sh` reports **0 missing**; confirm `schema_migrations` is complete;
   grep post-deploy logs for zero `relation "ŌĆ”" does not exist`. Money/entitlement paths depend on this.

4. **Build + full test suite must be confirmed in a clean environment.** The local build/test could
   not be brought fully green here due to corrupted `node_modules` (see honesty note). **Owner:**
   release eng. **Fix:** trigger a clean Vercel production build (clean `npm install`) and confirm
   `build` + `check:dist` pass; run the full `vitest`/`playwright` suite on a clean checkout (ideally
   via the new push-CI lane from Blocker 1/Prompt 37). The strong local evidence (typecheck+lint clean,
   1499 modules transformed, all prebuild audits green, parity green) says the code is build-sound ŌĆö this
   is a confirmation step, not expected to surface new defects.

### ­¤¤Ī Non-blocking follow-ups (burn down post-launch)

- **Feed-failure UX cluster** (P1): `/oracle`, `/smart-money`, `/pump-dashboard`, `/pumpfun`,
  `/pump-visualizer` keep stale data / spin instead of a recoverable error+retry. Copy `/pump-live`.
- **`/play/agent-wallet`** (P1): deploy a `BRIDGE_URL` or show an honest dev-only state.
- **`/avatar-artifact`** (P1): mobile/touch support.
- **`/create-agent`** (P1): clear the `#page-loading` veil + retry on module parse failure.
- **Blog footer regression** (P2): ~22/28 posts load `footer.css` but render no footer.
- **Data-table a11y** (P2): `/oracle`, `/activity`, `/leaderboard`, `/trending`, `/smart-money`.
- **Test-suite reliability** (RISK): split a fast deterministic lane from the slow network lane so CI
  signal is trustworthy (carried from the 06-18 audit; still open).

---

## 7. Rollback plan

| Trigger | Action |
|---|---|
| Bad deploy (errors, broken surface) | Vercel **Instant Rollback** to the previous production deployment (Vercel dashboard ŌåÆ Deployments ŌåÆ Promote previous). Frontend + API revert atomically. |
| Migration / schema regression | Migrations are **additive & idempotent** (`create table/index if not exists`, `add column if not exists`) ŌĆö forward-safe, so a code rollback does not require a schema rollback. If a destructive change ever ships, restore via Neon **PITR** to the pre-deploy timestamp. |
| Missing-table storm recurs | Re-run `npm run db:bootstrap` (or `scripts/restore-missing-tables.sh --apply` with `OLD_URL`/`NEW_URL`) against the canonical DB; both are idempotent and data-safe. |
| Payment/x402 incident | x402 has an always-on replay guard and server-side amount/recipient/mint verification; disable the affected paid endpoint via its feature gate while investigating ŌĆö settlement state is in-DB and auditable. |
| App-data corruption | Recover the affected daily snapshot from git history: `scripts/snapshot-export-day.sh <YYYY-MM-DD>`. |
| Code revert hygiene | Use a **neutral** revert message (never echo the reverted title ŌĆö CLAUDE.md). |

---

## 8. What this gate changed (this pass)

All edits are gate-greening / coin-policy hygiene; staged as **explicit paths only** (concurrent
agents are editing `main`). Not user-facing features ŌåÆ no `changelog.json` entry.

| File | Change | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `src/reasoning-ledger.js`, `src/vaults.js` | added `loading="lazy" decoding="async"` to two `<img>` | `check:images` strict was RED (2 offenders) ŌåÆ now PASS |
| `data/pages.json` | documented `/ibm/hello` (canonical IBM partnership page; no `added` date ŌĆö already shipped) | `audit-page-index --strict` was RED ŌåÆ now PASS |
| `scripts/audit-page-index.mjs` | added `/ibm/hello.live` to IGNORE (generated-from source variant of `/ibm/hello`) | same ŌĆö matches the existing A/B-alias convention |
| `docs/pumpfun-program/docs/FAQ.md`, `ŌĆ”/PUMP_SWAP_README.md`, `docs/ALL.md` | neutralized 3 foreign example mints (see ┬¦4) | coin-policy final scan ŌåÆ now CLEAN |
| `public/llms.txt`, `public/llms-full.txt`, `public/changelog.json`, `public/features.json`, `public/sitemap/index.html` | regenerated by `build:pages` (no new changelog entries) | consequence of documenting `/ibm/hello` |

> ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ The working tree also shows `package.json`, `scripts/rank-backlog.mjs`,
> `scripts/verify-task-completion.mjs` modified ŌĆö these are **concurrent-agent edits on `main`**, not
> part of this gate. Do not stage them with this pass.

---

## 9. Go / No-Go per area

| Area | Verdict |
|---|---|
| Coin policy | Ō£ģ **GO** (clean after remediation) |
| Security | Ō£ģ **GO** (0 open) |
| Money paths (code) | Ō£ģ **GO** |
| On-chain / Solana parity | Ō£ģ **GO** |
| Surfaces (UX) | Ō£ģ **GO** (no P0; P1/P2 polish tracked) |
| Legal | Ō£ģ **GO** |
| Observability / health / status | Ō£ģ **GO** |
| Backups / rollback | Ō£ģ **GO** |
| Lint / typecheck / static audits | Ō£ģ **GO** |
| **Branding lock** | ­¤ö┤ **NO-GO** (Blocker 1) |
| **Live MCP health** | ­¤ö┤ **NO-GO** (Blocker 2) |
| **Canonical DB migration state** | ­¤ö┤ **VERIFY** (Blocker 3) |
| **Build / test in clean CI** | ­¤¤Ī **CONFIRM** (Blocker 4) |
| **CI gating coverage** | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ **PARTIAL** (Prompt 37 ŌĆö add push-CI lane) |

## Final recommendation

**­¤¤Ī CONDITIONAL GO ŌĆö HOLD.** Clear Blockers 1ŌĆō4, then re-run `npm run smoke:mcp` (ŌåÆ 6/6),
`npx vitest run tests/branding.test.js` (ŌåÆ 0 fail), confirm the canonical DB has every migration
applied, and confirm a clean Vercel build + `check:dist` are green. On all-green, this flips to
**GO**. The platform's core (payments, security, on-chain, coin policy) is launch-grade; the held
items are closeable within hours, not days.

---

<a id="sec-167"></a>

> **Source:** `docs/layers.md` ┬Ę [Ōåæ table of contents](#table-of-contents)

# Layers

three.ws is split into four horizontal layers. Each layer can be used on its own. Each layer is independently replaceable as long as its contract with the rest of the system holds. This document describes what each layer owns, what it exports, and where to extend it.

For the overall request flow, see [How three.ws works](./how-it-works.md). For internal module names and paths, see [Architecture overview](./architecture.md).

---

## The contract

```
ŌöīŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöÉ
Ōöé  Embed layer                                 Ōöé
Ōöé   <agent-3d> ┬Ę widgets ┬Ę iframe ┬Ę SDKs       Ōöé
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöż
Ōöé  Identity layer  (optional)                  Ōöé
Ōöé   ERC-8004 ┬Ę Metaplex Core ┬Ę IPFS ┬Ę SIWE/SIWSŌöé
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöż
Ōöé  Agent layer                                 Ōöé
Ōöé   LLM runtime ┬Ę skills ┬Ę memory ┬Ę empathy    Ōöé
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöż
Ōöé  Viewer layer                                Ōöé
Ōöé   three.js ┬Ę GLTFLoader ┬Ę AnimationMixer     Ōöé
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöś
```

The layers communicate through one event bus (`agent-protocol`). No layer calls another layer's methods directly. This is the load-bearing invariant ŌĆö keep it intact and any layer can be replaced.

---

## Layer 1 ŌĆö Viewer

**What it owns:** rendering, loading, animation, camera, lights, post-processing.

**Doesn't know about:** agents, brains, wallets, manifests, the protocol bus.

The Viewer is pure three.js. It can be used as a standalone glTF inspector with no agent at all ŌĆö and is, in the Turntable and Animation Gallery widgets.

Concretely:

- `WebGLRenderer` configured for `devicePixelRatio`, demand-rendered (`viewer.invalidate()`), idle frames skipped.
- `GLTFLoader` with `DRACOLoader`, `KTX2Loader`, and `MeshoptDecoder` attached. Decoder URLs are version-pinned to the bundled three.js.
- `OrbitControls`, auto-disabled when an embedded glTF camera is selected.
- `AnimationMixer` plus a per-clip action-state map.
- HDRI environment, exposure / tone mapping controls, optional helpers (grid, axes, wireframe, skeleton).
- Multi-file resolution via `LoadingManager.setURLModifier()` so drag-and-drop `scene.gltf + scene.bin + textures/...` works locally without uploads.

**Extension points the next layer uses:**

- `viewer._afterAnimateHooks` ŌĆö per-frame callbacks (emotion decay, tweens)
- `viewer.invalidate()` ŌĆö request a render
- `viewer.content` / `viewer.scene` / `viewer.mixer`
- `viewer.animationManager` ŌĆö external clip lazy-loading

If you only need a glTF viewer with no AI, importing `@three-ws/avatar` gives you this layer with a clean programmatic API (`loadAvatar()`, `playAnimation()`, etc.). The hosted Turntable and Animation Gallery widgets at `/w/<id>` are also pure viewer-layer.

---

## Layer 2 ŌĆö Agent

**What it owns:** the LLM tool-loop, skills, memory, the empathy / emotion blend, the protocol bus.

**Doesn't know about:** how the LLM is hosted, where the wallet lives, which embed surface is showing it.

Modules:

- **`agent-protocol.js`** ŌĆö singleton `EventTarget` bus. Every action is a `CustomEvent`. 200-event ring buffer (`protocol.history`) for debugging.
- **`runtime/index.js`** ŌĆö LLM tool-loop. Builds the system prompt from `instructions.md` + memory + skills, calls the provider, dispatches tool calls, feeds results back. Capped at `MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS = 8`.
- **`runtime/scene.js`** ŌĆö `SceneController`, the only bridge between agent intent and three.js reality: `playClipByName`, `playAnimationByHint`, `lookAt`, `setExpression`, `loadGLB`, `loadClip`, `moveTo`.
- **`runtime/tools.js`** ŌĆö built-in tools (`wave`, `lookAt`, `play_clip`, `setExpression`, `speak`, `remember`) and stage-scoped tools (`observe_agents`, `say_to_agent`).
- **`runtime/speech.js`** ŌĆö TTS (ElevenLabs / Web Speech API) and STT (browser `SpeechRecognition`). Silent no-op where unavailable.
- **`agent-skills.js`** ŌĆö skill registry. Skills are dynamically loaded bundles (`SKILL.md`, `tools.json`, `handlers.js`). Trust modes (`any` / `owned-only` / `whitelist`).
- **`agent-memory.js`** ŌĆö four-type memory store (`user`, `feedback`, `project`, `reference`). File-based memories live as frontmatter `.md` files indexed by `MEMORY.md` plus an append-only `timeline.jsonl`.
- **`agent-avatar.js`** ŌĆö the empathy layer. Translates bus events into a continuous emotion blend (neutral, concern, celebration, patience, curiosity, empathy) and drives morph targets, head tilt, and gaze.

The full agent layer can run against any three.js scene, not just our viewer ŌĆö `SceneController` only needs the standard handles.

**Event bus vocabulary:** see [architecture.md ┬¦2](./architecture.md#2-the-event-bus-agent-protocoljs).

---

## Layer 3 ŌĆö Identity

**What it owns:** persistent identity across sessions, devices, and embed hosts. Wallet auth, on-chain registration, IPFS-pinned manifests, signed action diary.

**Doesn't know about:** which avatar is loaded, what the LLM is doing.

**Entirely optional.** Most agents work fine without it. Removing this layer drops you back to a local-only experience ŌĆö the agent still runs, but it has no persistent identity.

Modules:

- **`agent-identity.js`** ŌĆö the passport + diary. Stable agent ID, owner address, signed action history. Backed by `localStorage` (cache) and `/api/agents/<id>` (canonical). Listens on the protocol bus and POSTs `speak` / `skill-done` / `validate` / `sign` events to `/api/agent-actions`.
- **`memory/`** ŌĆö `local`, `ipfs`, `encrypted-ipfs`, or `none`. Declared on the manifest.
- **`erc8004/`** ŌĆö on-chain registries. `IdentityRegistry` (mints agent token with `tokenURI`), `ReputationRegistry` (signed feedback), `ValidationRegistry` (validation report hashes). Per-chain addresses in `erc8004/abi.js`.
- **`solana-agent/`** ŌĆö Solana counterpart. Metaplex Core asset mint + Solana Attestation Service (SAS) attestations.
- **`auth/` + `wallet/`** ŌĆö SIWE / SIWS for backend mutations; Privy for email/social ŌåÆ embedded wallet onboarding; session cookies after wallet proof.

Per-chain registry addresses, ABIs, and helpers ship as `@three-ws/sdk`. ENS attestations are also handled here ŌĆö see [register-onchain.md](./tutorials/register-onchain.md).

---

## Layer 4 ŌĆö Embed

**What it owns:** the public face. The web component, widgets, iframe surfaces, the CDN bundle, the SPA, edge routing.

**Doesn't know about:** the contents of the manifest, what the LLM said, what the avatar is doing ŌĆö only that they should be presented somewhere.

Modules:

- **`element.js`** ŌĆö `<agent-3d>` custom element. Lazy-boots via `IntersectionObserver` (unless `eager`). Enforces origin allowlist. Exposes attributes for `body`, `brain`, `agent-id`, `manifest`, `mode` (`inline` / `floating` / `section` / `fullscreen`), `voice`, and more. Public methods: `say()`, `ask()`, `installSkill()`, `expressEmotion()`, `play()`, `lookAt()`, `dispose()`.
- **`agent-stage.js`** ŌĆö `<agent-stage>` for hosting multiple agents in one shared scene. See [multi-agent.md](./multi-agent.md).
- **`widget-types.js`** ŌĆö the five widget variants: Turntable, Animation Gallery, Talking Agent, ERC-8004 Passport, Hotspot Tour.
- **`lib.js`** ŌĆö the CDN entry. Imports the element, registers it, re-exports the public surface (`defineElement`, `Agent3DElement`, `AgentStageElement`, helpers).
- **`embed-action-bridge.js`** ŌĆö the `postMessage` protocol. Iframe widgets and the web component speak the same dialect, so host pages can drive either with the same code.
- **`app.js`** ŌĆö the main SPA. URL routing via hash (`#model=`, `#agent=`, `#kiosk=`) and query (`?agent=`) params.
- **`vercel.json`** ŌĆö edge routing. Clean URLs (`/agent/<id>`, `/agent/<id>/embed`, `/a/<chainId>/<agentId>`, `/w/<widget-id>`) map to the right HTML entries.

The embed layer's contract is the only thing third-party developers see directly. Keep its API surface small and stable; changes here are breaking changes per [SemVer](./changelog.md#versioning-policy).

---

## Cross-cutting: the protocol bus

Every layer above the Viewer subscribes to the same `agent-protocol` event bus.

- **Avatar layer** listens for `speak`, `gesture`, `skill-done`, `skill-error`, `validate`, `load-*` to pick emotion blends.
- **Identity layer** listens for `speak`, `remember`, `sign`, `skill-done`, `validate`, `load-end` and persists them.
- **Chat UI** listens for `speak` (render bubbles) and `voice:transcript` (live STT).
- **Memory** listens for `remember`.
- **`postMessage` bridge** mirrors a curated subset to host pages.

None of these modules know about each other. They subscribe by event type. That's the whole abstraction ŌĆö and the reason any one of them can be ripped out and replaced.

If you're building a custom layer (a 2D sprite avatar, a vector-store memory backend, a different LLM provider), you only need to listen to and emit the same event vocabulary. Everything else is wiring.

---

## Build targets

The same source tree produces three independent builds:

- **App** ŌĆö `npm run build`. The full SPA into `dist/`. Editor, agent pages, discover, studio, PWA manifest. Multi-page Rollup config with a Vercel-style dev middleware.
- **Library** ŌĆö `npm run build:lib`. `src/lib.js` ŌåÆ `dist-lib/agent-3d.js` (ES module + UMD). Three.js and ethers stay bundled ŌĆö the file is intentionally self-contained so a single `<script type="module">` is the only thing a third party needs.
- **Artifact** ŌĆö `vite.config.artifact.js`. A zero-dependency bundle for Claude artifact embeds. Inlined everything, no external script tags, no dynamic imports.

Versioned CDN bundles are published at `/agent-3d/<version>/agent-3d.js`. Use `latest` for auto-updates or pin to a version for stability. See [changelog.md](./changelog.md) for release history.

---

## What's next

- **[Architecture overview](./architecture.md)** ŌĆö module-by-module deep dive
- **[Agent system](./agent-system.md)** ŌĆö runtime, skills, memory in detail
- **[Embedding](./embedding.md)** ŌĆö the public face of the embed layer
- **[SDK reference](./sdk.md)** ŌĆö `@three-ws/sdk` and friends

---

<a id="sec-168"></a>

# Listings & Distribution

three.ws is open source and self-hostable. It is also distributed through cloud marketplaces (for enterprise procurement) and indexed in ecosystem dApp directories (for community discovery). This page tracks every official listing.

If you maintain a marketplace, registry, or directory and want three.ws listed, open an issue at [github.com/nirholas/three.ws/issues](https://github.com/nirholas/three.ws/issues) or reach out via the contacts on [three.ws](https://three.ws).

---

## Cloud Marketplaces

Cloud marketplaces are the procurement flow enterprises use to acquire software through their existing cloud account, with consolidated billing, compliance, and support.

| Cloud | Status | Links |
|---|---|---|
| **AWS** Marketplace | Live | three.ws is an AWS Partner. Subscribe via AWS Marketplace to consolidate billing on your AWS account; subscriptions auto-issue an x402 API key via the marketplace entitlement service. See [aws-marketplace.md](./aws-marketplace.md). |
| **Alibaba Cloud** International Marketplace | Live | [Product listing](https://marketplace.alibabacloud.com/products/56724001/sgcmfw00036800.html) ┬Ę [Storefront](https://marketplace.alibabacloud.com/store/3247293.html) ┬Ę [Announcement](/blog/three-ws-on-alibaba-cloud-marketplace.html) ┬Ę [Marketplace blog feature](https://marketplace.alibabacloud.com/doc/blog/detail/mplace-sgcmfw00036800.html) |
| **Google Cloud** Marketplace | Open to partnership | three.ws runs on WebGL, Vercel edge, and EVM ŌĆö a natural fit for Vertex AI and GCP's global CDN. Reach out for co-listing, credits, and joint GTM. |
| **Microsoft Azure** Marketplace | On roadmap | Targeted alongside the AWS/Alibaba rollout. |

---

## Ecosystem Directories

Ecosystem directories are the discovery surfaces that L1/L2 communities, chain foundations, and dApp explorers publish to help users find vetted projects on their stack.

| Directory | Status | Listing details |
|---|---|---|
| **BNB Chain ┬Ę Dappbay** | Live | [dappbay.bnbchain.org/detail/three](https://dappbay.bnbchain.org/detail/three) ŌĆö categories: *AI Agent Launchpad ┬Ę AI Data ┬Ę AI Infra*. Announcement: [three.ws Listed on BNB Chain's Dappbay Directory](/blog/three-ws-on-bnb-chain-dappbay.html). |

Dappbay is BNB Chain's official dApp directory. The listing is about distribution and discovery into BNB Chain's AI dApp audience ŌĆö three.ws still settles agent payments on Solana, Base, and Polygon via the chain-agnostic [x402 protocol](./x402.md).

---

## Media & Content Partners

| Partner | Status | Links |
|---|---|---|
| **IBM Community** | Live | [Embodied, Intelligent, and Self-Custodied: three.ws and the 3D AI Agent Stack](https://community.ibm.com/community/user/blogs/nich8/2026/06/08/3d-ai-web3-just-converge-threews-shipped-the-whole) ŌĆö IBM Community blog covering the convergence of 3D, AI agents, and web3 in the three.ws stack. |
| **HackerNoon** | Live | [hackernoon.com/u/three-ws](https://hackernoon.com/u/three-ws) ŌĆö three.ws posts auto-import from the RSS feed. Announcement: [three.ws Partners with HackerNoon](/news/partnered-with-hackernoon) |
| **Alibaba Cloud Marketplace Blog** | Live | [Editorial feature](https://marketplace.alibabacloud.com/doc/blog/detail/mplace-sgcmfw00036800.html) ŌĆö Alibaba Cloud Marketplace published an editorial introducing three.ws. Announcement: [three.ws Featured on the Alibaba Cloud Marketplace Blog](/blog/three-ws-featured-on-alibaba-cloud-marketplace-blog) |

HackerNoon is one of the world's largest independent tech publications, read by millions of developers and founders monthly. Every three.ws announcement is pulled automatically from [`three.ws/rss/announcements.xml`](https://three.ws/rss/announcements.xml) into the HackerNoon drafts queue, then published with canonical URLs pointing back to three.ws. See [syndication setup](/docs/syndication#hackernoon) for technical details.

---

## Open Source

The full stack is on GitHub:

- **three.ws** ŌĆö [github.com/nirholas/three.ws](https://github.com/nirholas/three.ws)
- Mirrored at ŌĆö [github.com/nirholas/3D-Agent](https://github.com/nirholas/3D-Agent)

The codebase includes the `<agent-3d>` browser component, the Solana minting SDK, the x402 payment SDK, the ERC-8004 wallet integration, and the agent-payments SDK used by third-party developers.

---

## RSS & Announcements

Every listing is announced through:

- **RSS** ŌĆö [`https://three.ws/rss/announcements.xml`](https://three.ws/rss/announcements.xml)
- **News index** ŌĆö [`https://three.ws/news/`](https://three.ws/news/)
- **Blog** ŌĆö [`https://three.ws/blog/`](https://three.ws/blog/)

Subscribe via RSS to track new listings, integrations, and protocol updates.
